sessions.notes:
Evermore wilt:
- Duur gevoel, snel, strak.
- Gelijk de artiesten zien bij openen site
- De artiesten grid op voorpagina moet blijven, maar moet veel strakker
- Logo’s van bedrijven op mooie manier verwerken
- Mooie animaties verwerken (bv. Met de artiesten cards)
- Info waar evermore voor staat in animaties verwerken.
- Willen goed balans tussen: In 1 scroll alles zien vs. Duidelijkheid houden en niet overzicht kwijtraken

Ze hebben:
- Visual content van de artiesten
- Branding folder
- porpora font
- Logos in al formats

Concurrenten zijn:
- https://www.wmeagency.com
- https://www.teamwass.com

Kosten
- 40 euro per maand voor hele pakket. Vindt Luuk een nette prijs, zolang echt alles is inbegrepen.
- Met Lars overleggen of er extra kosten bijkomen voor complexe automations, maar we zijn het erover eens dat we hier samen uitkomen en een win-win situatie kunnen maken.
- Staan er voor open om powered by / made by Alive onderaan de site toe te voegen.

Next steps Evermore:
- Ze sturen ons:
- Webflow templates, met korte uitleg waarom deze zijn gekozen
- Branding content

Next steps Alive:
- Gieten evermore site in nieuw design gebaseerd op hun input

1
Mooie template, laten er even samen doorheen lopen, en de tweaks bespreken.

- Op de homepage, ipv work, artiesten
- En dan 4 artiesten naast elkaar, en een groep van 9/10 laten zien, en daaronder een knop met bekijk alle.
- De gradient animaties op de homepage kunnen hetzelfde blijven. Misschien later aanpassen naar artiesten foto carousel.
￼
- Dat laadscherm met logo op bij de home page is nice. Zwart, met logo in hun kleur (mint).
- Achtergrond. Beige = mint. Zwart = hun zwart.
￼
- Meeste van hun klanten zitten op hun laptop (dus belangrijker dan mobile).
- Logo’s gedeelte hetzelfde houden, logos mogen wat sneller en wellicht met je muis kunnen laten bewegen.
- Contact (top vd homepage) zelfde houden, maar dan een contact icoon ipv foto van 1 iemand.
- In het side menu: YouTube ipv twitter, rest hetzelfde.
- Menu items overnemen zoals op hun oude evermore website.
- Al die oranje bolletjes met getallen kunnen weg uit template.
￼
- Die animaties in about us kunnen hetzelfde.
- Services kunnen core values worden
- ￼
- De laatst gemaakte docu kan op de video plek op de homepage
￼
- FAQ kunnen we laten staan.
- Start your project with allure, dan gezichten met artiesten.
- Klikken op bekijk alle artiesten.
- Work/selected work wordt artiesten,
- Meanwhile.nl
- Eerst roster als losse namen, en dan een grid.
￼
- De pagina van 1 artiest, kan meer in hun oorspronkelijk
- Our studio, wordt ‘about us’
- Blog kunnen we skippen
- Jobs kan evt. In het blog format
- Contact pagina ziet er goed uit, zelfde houden

2
Nog even de kosten besproken zoals hieronder:
- 40 eu per maand
- Complete website
- Toegang tot de terminal, met 20 eu aan credits (kunnen ze lovable opzeggen)
- Dashboard overzetten
- Laten we die automations hierbuiten houden
Luuk begrijpt dat we niet voor deze prijs allerlei automations gaan inbouwen, en verwacht dit ook later niet van ons.

insights.jobMotivation: I want an affordable way to test my idea online
insights.jobOutcome: so I can save money for actual product development.
insights.text: For a startup, spending $50/month on a website feels steep when I'm still validating the idea.
insights.jobSituation: When I'm trying to build an MVP landing page on a tight budget

insights.jobMotivation: I want them to immediately see the quality and style of our work
insights.jobOutcome: so they feel confident reaching out for a project.
insights.text: Being able to showcase our work visually is crucial for attracting new clients.
insights.jobSituation: When potential clients visit our portfolio

insights.jobMotivation: I want to know what features are standard vs premium
insights.jobOutcome: so I don't get surprised after signing up.
insights.text: I assumed the starter plan had custom domains because the competitor's basic plan does.
insights.jobSituation: When I first looked at your pricing tiers

insights.jobMotivation: I want to get online and start getting feedback immediately
insights.jobOutcome: so I can iterate based on real customer responses.
insights.text: Speed is everything - I'd rather have a decent site in 30 minutes than a perfect one in 2 weeks.
insights.jobSituation: When I'm launching a new product experiment

insights.jobMotivation: I want editing to work smoothly on any device
insights.jobOutcome: so I'm not forced to wait until I have my laptop.
insights.text: On mobile, the edit button was too small and hard to find - I gave up and used my laptop.
insights.jobSituation: When I want to quickly update a project description from my phone

insights.jobMotivation: I want to see some concrete options
insights.jobOutcome: so I can choose quickly and tweak instead of inventing.
insights.text: I would rather pick from 3-4 starting layouts instead of writing everything from scratch.
insights.jobSituation: When I'm trying a new site builder

insights.jobMotivation: I want pixel-perfect control over every design element
insights.jobOutcome: so we can match their existing brand identity exactly.
insights.text: We need more control over typography, spacing, and custom CSS for client branding.
insights.jobSituation: When we're building sites for clients with strict brand guidelines

insights.jobMotivation: I want to get started without overthinking the content
insights.jobOutcome: so I don't feel stuck before I even see a first version.
insights.text: This first screen asks for a lot of text; I'm not sure what to write here.
insights.jobSituation: When I first try to generate a site for my studio

insights.jobMotivation: I want the simplest possible way to make basic edits
insights.jobOutcome: so I can spend my time running the business instead.
insights.text: I just need to update our daily menu and opening hours - nothing fancy.
insights.jobSituation: When I need to change business information on the website

insights.jobMotivation: I want to clearly see what's included in each tier
insights.jobOutcome: so I can make an informed decision without contacting support.
insights.text: The pricing page doesn't make it obvious which plan includes custom domains.
insights.jobSituation: When I'm comparing plans to decide what to subscribe to

insights.jobMotivation: I want to get online and start getting feedback immediately
insights.jobOutcome: so I can iterate based on real customer responses.
insights.text: Speed is everything - I'd rather have a decent site in 30 minutes than a perfect one in 2 weeks.
insights.jobSituation: When I'm launching a new product experiment

experiments.hypothesis: Because startups need affordable validation (Insight ins_14), introducing a $15/month 'Validation' tier with basic features will capture startup customers who would otherwise not sign up.
experiments.title: Introduce $15/month validation tier for startups
experiments.metric: Total monthly revenue from startup segment
experiments.changeDescription: Create a new pricing tier at $15/month with: 1 site, basic templates, community support, and Alive subdomain only (no custom domain). Position it as 'perfect for testing your idea.'
experiments.problem: Early-stage startups find the $50/month price too high for MVP validation.

experiments.hypothesis: Because customers like seeing real results (Insight ins_6), adding a 'Customer Highlights' section with 2-3 brief success stories will increase homepage-to-trial conversion.
experiments.title: Show customer success examples on homepage
experiments.metric: Overall homepage-to-trial conversion rate
experiments.changeDescription: Add a section above the footer showing 3 customer cards: photo, name, business type, and a one-sentence quote about their result (e.g., 'Our bakery gets more online orders since we updated daily specials on our Alive site').
experiments.problem: We have positive customer feedback but don't showcase it prominently to build trust.

experiments.hypothesis: Because users are confused by the word 'prompt' (Insights ins_1 and ins_3), we believe that replacing it with plain language will increase the percentage of visitors who start generating a site.
experiments.decision: Ship it - the clearer language significantly improved conversion. Keep monitoring for a week to ensure it holds.
experiments.title: Hero text without the word 'prompt'
experiments.metric: % visitors who click the primary "Start your site" CTA
experiments.changeDescription: Update hero copy from 'Create a site from one prompt' to 'Describe your idea and get a complete website in minutes.'
experiments.problem: Several users do not understand the word 'prompt' in the hero and feel uncertain about what Alive does.

experiments.hypothesis: Because studio users prefer choosing from layouts instead of writing from scratch (Insights ins_3 and ins_4), we believe that offering 3-4 starting templates will increase the percentage of studio signups who reach a first generated site.
experiments.title: Template-first onboarding flow for small studios
experiments.metric: % studio signups who reach first generated site
experiments.changeDescription: Add a step to onboarding where studio users can pick from 4 predefined layouts (portfolio, agency, product showcase, minimal), each with sample content they can later edit.
experiments.problem: Studio users feel blocked by having to write a lot of text before they can see anything.

experiments.hypothesis: Because non-technical users need clear visual cues (Insight ins_5), we believe that adding subtle borders on hover will help them find editable areas faster.
experiments.decision: Shipped - users found edit areas 50% faster. Also reduced support questions about 'how to change text' by 30%.
experiments.title: Highlight editing areas with subtle borders on hover
experiments.metric: Average time to first edit (seconds)
experiments.changeDescription: When users hover over any editable text or image area, show a light blue border with a small 'Edit' label in the top-right corner.
experiments.problem: Non-technical users don't know where to click to edit content on their site.

experiments.hypothesis: Because local business owners need simple updates (Insight ins_12), providing a pre-built 'Business Basics' template with menu, hours, and contact sections will increase local business signup-to-publish rate.
experiments.title: Create 'Business Basics' template for local shops
experiments.metric: % local business signups who publish a site within 48 hours
experiments.changeDescription: Create a new template specifically for local businesses with sections for: business hours, menu/services, location map, contact form. Pre-populate with placeholder content that's easy to replace.
experiments.problem: Local business owners want simple sites but feel overwhelmed by customization options.

experiments.hypothesis: Because agencies need advanced customization (Insight ins_13), adding custom CSS editing as a Pro feature will attract agency customers and reduce churn.
experiments.decision: Scrapped - This takes us too far from our core value proposition of simplicity. Agencies needing this level of control should use Webflow or custom code. We'll focus on our strength: speed and ease for non-technical users.
experiments.title: Add advanced CSS customization for Pro plan
experiments.metric: % agency signups who upgrade to Pro plan
experiments.changeDescription: Add a 'Custom CSS' panel in the editor for Pro plan users, allowing them to write custom styles with syntax highlighting and live preview.
experiments.problem: Design agencies need pixel-perfect control over typography and spacing for client branding.

experiments.hypothesis: Because users value speed over perfection (Insights ins_2 and ins_15), providing a 30-minute video showing the fastest path to publishing will increase day-1 publish rate.
experiments.title: Add 30-minute quick-start video tutorial
experiments.metric: % new signups who publish within 24 hours
experiments.changeDescription: Record and embed a 30-minute 'Launch Your Site Today' tutorial showing: template selection, basic customization, domain setup, and publishing. Feature it prominently in onboarding email and dashboard.
experiments.problem: Startups and freelancers want to launch fast but don't know the most efficient workflow.

experiments.hypothesis: Because mobile editing is difficult (Insight ins_10), increasing touch target sizes and adding visual feedback will reduce mobile editing friction and increase mobile edit completion rate.
experiments.title: Improve mobile editor touch targets
experiments.metric: % mobile sessions that complete at least one edit
experiments.changeDescription: Increase edit button size on mobile to minimum 44x44px. Add subtle pulsing animation on first mobile visit to guide users. Show toast confirmation after successful edits.
experiments.problem: Mobile users struggle to find and tap edit buttons due to small touch targets.

experiments.hypothesis: Because freelancers want to launch quickly (Insight ins_2), explicitly promising '60 minutes to a live site' will increase trial signups from freelancers.
experiments.title: Add 'Quick Start in 60 Minutes' promise to homepage
experiments.metric: % freelancer visitors who start trial
experiments.changeDescription: Add a badge or callout near the hero that says 'From idea to live site in under 60 minutes' with a small timer icon.
experiments.problem: Freelancers value speed but we don't explicitly promise how fast they can launch.

experiments.hypothesis: Because users can't easily compare plan features (Insights ins_8 and ins_9), adding a clear comparison table will reduce confusion and increase conversion from free to paid.
experiments.title: Add feature comparison table to pricing page
experiments.metric: % visitors who upgrade from starter to premium plan
experiments.changeDescription: Add a detailed feature comparison table showing all plans side-by-side with checkmarks for included features. Highlight custom domains, storage limits, and support level for each tier.
experiments.problem: Users are confused about what features are included in each pricing tier.

experiments.hypothesis: Because freelancers want to launch quickly (Insight ins_2), explicitly promising '60 minutes to a live site' will increase trial signups from freelancers.
experiments.title: Add 'Quick Start in 60 Minutes' promise to homepage
experiments.metric: % freelancer visitors who start trial
experiments.changeDescription: Add a badge or callout near the hero that says 'From idea to live site in under 60 minutes' with a small timer icon.
experiments.problem: Freelancers value speed but we don't explicitly promise how fast they can launch.

issues.title: kopieer lucky flowchart framework naar roadmap en userflows

blueprints.oneLiner: Integrated booking system with automated reminders and post-service follow-up for local service providers
blueprints.name: Local Service Bookings + Reminders + Follow-up
blueprints.proofSummary: Used by 2 customers. 60% no-show reduction through reminders, 25% repeat booking increase.
blueprints.useCase: For hairdressers, therapists, consultants, and service businesses needing reliable booking and customer engagement

blueprints.oneLiner: First customer delivery flow: take a lead from discovery to a live website + working lead workflow.
blueprints.name: Customer First-Case

outcomes.notes: No routing errors or manual fixes needed
80% of first customers reuse this blueprint

blueprints.internalNotes: This blueprint is the foundation for all first-time customer engagements. Focus on clarity, speed, and capturing learnings for iteration. See User Flows tab for operational details.
blueprints.proofSummary: Tested with 3 first customers (Studio Flora, Pixel & Clay, GrowthLabs). Average time from qualified lead to live: 7 days. 100% form submission routing success.
blueprints.useCase: Complete operational blueprint for delivering first customer engagements with Alive. Covers the entire journey from qualified lead through discovery, build, QA, go-live, and follow-up. Establishes reusable patterns for future customer implementations.

blueprints.oneLiner: Complete lead capture, qualification, and CRM synchronization for B2B SaaS products
blueprints.name: B2B Startup Lead Flow + CRM Sync
blueprints.proofSummary: Used by 3 customers. 40% increase in qualified leads, 50% reduction in manual follow-up.
blueprints.useCase: For early-stage B2B companies needing to capture, qualify, and nurture enterprise leads with automated follow-up

blueprints.oneLiner: Beautiful portfolio gallery with project inquiry forms and automated qualification workflow
blueprints.name: Portfolio + Project Inquiries + Qualification
blueprints.proofSummary: Used by 5+ customers. 3x increase in qualified project inquiries.
blueprints.useCase: For freelancers, design studios, and creative professionals showcasing work and generating qualified leads

blueprints.oneLiner: Beautiful portfolio gallery with project inquiry forms and automated qualification workflow
blueprints.name: Portfolio + Project Inquiries + Qualification
blueprints.proofSummary: Used by 5+ customers. 3x increase in qualified project inquiries.
blueprints.useCase: For freelancers, design studios, and creative professionals showcasing work and generating qualified leads

steps: User views portfolio project and clicks 'Inquire'
Form asks about project scope, budget, timeline
Qualification criteria evaluate budget vs. service tier
Acknowledgment email sent with next steps
Inquiry stored in Notion database
Freelancer notified with quality score
blueprint_workflows.name: Project Inquiry Flow
blueprint_workflows.description: User submits inquiry about portfolio project, gets qualified and routed to freelancer

steps: User submits form with company info, use case, budget
Validation checks required fields
Lead qualification rules evaluate company size and fit
Contact record created in Hubspot
Auto-response email sent
Sales team notified via Slack
blueprint_workflows.name: Lead Capture Flow
blueprint_workflows.description: Captures leads via form, qualifies based on criteria, syncs to Hubspot

steps: User selects service and available time slot
Booking details captured (name, email, phone, notes)
Confirmation sent via email and SMS
Calendar event created
Automated reminder: 7 days before
Automated reminder: 24 hours before
Follow-up survey sent 1 day after appointment
blueprint_workflows.name: Booking & Reminder Flow
blueprint_workflows.description: User books service, receives confirmation, gets reminders, and post-service follow-up

steps: User selects service and available time slot
Booking details captured (name, email, phone, notes)
Confirmation sent via email and SMS
Calendar event created
Automated reminder: 7 days before
Automated reminder: 24 hours before
Follow-up survey sent 1 day after appointment
blueprint_workflows.name: Booking & Reminder Flow
blueprint_workflows.description: User books service, receives confirmation, gets reminders, and post-service follow-up

steps: User views portfolio project and clicks 'Inquire'
Form asks about project scope, budget, timeline
Qualification criteria evaluate budget vs. service tier
Acknowledgment email sent with next steps
Inquiry stored in Notion database
Freelancer notified with quality score
blueprint_workflows.name: Project Inquiry Flow
blueprint_workflows.description: User submits inquiry about portfolio project, gets qualified and routed to freelancer

steps: User submits form with company info, use case, budget
Validation checks required fields
Lead qualification rules evaluate company size and fit
Contact record created in Hubspot
Auto-response email sent
Sales team notified via Slack
blueprint_workflows.name: Lead Capture Flow
blueprint_workflows.description: Captures leads via form, qualifies based on criteria, syncs to Hubspot

blueprint_integrations.notes: Custom properties for budget tier and qualification score
blueprint_integrations.purpose: Sync qualified leads and manage pipeline

blueprint_integrations.notes: Uses Zapier to trigger Twilio SMS

blueprint_integrations.purpose: Sync bookings and manage provider availability
Store and manage all project inquiries

Custom properties for budget tier and qualification score
blueprint_integrations.purpose: Sync qualified leads and manage pipeline

dataEffects: Customer: status updates, goal, nextStep, nextStepDue
Signals: created from sessions, triaged outcomes
Blueprint updates: add new reusable pattern notes + variables
notifications: Visitor confirmation email after form submission (if implemented)
Internal notification to owner when new lead arrives (email/task)
Follow-up task created if lead not responded within X days (optional)
successMetrics: Time from lead submission → owner notified (target: < 2 minutes)
% leads correctly routed (target: 100%, no manual fix needed)
Reuse: whether a new pattern was added to blueprint library

states.meaning: Lead is accepted and V1 scope agreed
states.exitWhen: When assets and workflow scope confirmed

states.meaning: V1 site and primary workflow being built
states.exitWhen: When preview link ready + workflow passes QA

sideEffects: Schedule review/live build session if needed

states.ui: 'Ready to Launch' status with launch button
'Live' badge with link to live site
Grayed out with 'Not a fit' note

blueprint_user_flows.name: First-Case: Lead → Live website + Lead workflow
preconditions: Customer exists in Customers with status = lead or active
At least one Session exists ('Discovery call')
At least 3 Signals captured from discovery (request/objection/constraint/urgency/bug)
Scope for V1 agreed: pages + one primary workflow (lead OR booking)
outOfScope: Advanced CRM enrichment beyond lead capture
Fully custom design system per customer

happyPath.text: Create/confirm Customer record with goal, constraints, and next step due date.
Run Discovery Session; capture notes and Signals.
Convert key Signals into: Playbook items (messaging/process), Issues (bugs/known tasks), or Experiment (uncertain improvements).
Choose a starting Blueprint (or 'Customer First-Case' if none) and define V1 scope: Pages (Home, Services, About, Contact minimum) and Primary workflow (Lead flow: form → routing → follow-up).
Collect assets (logo, copy, references) and confirm brand tone.
Build V1 website structure in Alive; publish preview link.
Implement lead workflow: Visitor submits form → System validates → Routes to target tool (email/CRM) → Creates follow-up task.
System sends confirmation email to visitor.
Run internal QA checklist (form works, notifications work, routing works).
Live-build session with customer; iterate on copy/layout.
Go live; confirm production domain, hosting, SEO basics.
Close loop: Customer status → active, Next step set (first-week check-in).
Capture reusable patterns and update blueprint notes.

blueprint_user_flows.trigger: A lead agrees to proceed with a website redesign/migration on Alive (post-discovery).

edgeCases.expected: Show validation error, no routing, no follow-up task created
Log error, create fallback task, notify owner immediately
Create new issue(s), explicitly re-confirm V1 scope with customer
Track 'In Review' until explicit approval received from primary contact

decisions.ifFalse: Mark 'Not a fit' + close with playbook response; set customer status to 'lost'
decisions.condition: Is the customer qualified for Alive V1?

decisions.ifFalse: Create minimal custom variant; log new pattern requirement in Issues

decisions.ifTrue: Swap primary workflow to booking; keep lead form optional
decisions.ifFalse: Proceed with lead workflow as primary
decisions.condition: Does customer require booking flow instead of lead flow?

decisions.ifTrue: Create P0 issue(s) and delay go-live until resolved

decisions.ifFalse: Schedule live build session and revise

blueprint_user_flows.goal: Turn a qualified lead into a live Alive website with a working lead automation (intake → routing → follow-up), while capturing reusable patterns for future blueprints.
